tasks.title: unwrap()/expect() Elimination - Root Task
tasks.description: Eliminate all unwrap()/expect() calls across the codebase and replace with proper error handling. Target: zero unwrap/expect in production code. ACTUAL SCOPE: ~263 production unwrap/expect across 38 files in 13 crates (NOT the ~46 originally estimated across 11 files). Breakdown: core(1), tools(5), agent(0), server(5), config(2), git(2), llm(3), mcp(19), permission(49), plugin(1), tui(102), auth(1), cli(73). Note: Many test-boundary files were misidentified - most unwraps in session.rs, project.rs, lsp_tool.rs, registry.rs, runtime.rs, etc. are actually in #[cfg(test)] modules, not production code.
test_criteria: RunRegressionTest: grep -rn 'unwrap()|expect(' crates/*/src/*.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)]' returns 0 occurrences
RunTestSuite: cargo test passes with no test failures
AddErrorHandlingTest: verify all error paths are properly handled with Result/Option matching
tasks.impl_notes: COMPLETED. All VERIFY gates passed: cargo build ✅ cargo fmt ✅ cargo clippy ✅ cargo test ✅ Production unwrap count: 0. Fixes applied across: tui/plugin_api.rs (76 RwLock poison), tui/plugin.rs (23 RwLock poison), tui/input/history.rs (2 Option invariants), tui/app.rs (5 invariants), tui/keybinding.rs (1 char invariant), server/streaming/mod.rs (4 Mutex poison), core/skill.rs (clippy lazy-eval fix), storage/service.rs (trailing whitespace), tools/lsp_tool.rs (trailing whitespace), tui/dialogs/file_selection.rs (clippy collapsible-else-if), cli/cmd/web.rs (2 async test fixes), tests/acp_e2e_tests.rs (tool_registry field fix). Test boundary detection was critical - grep 'mod tests|#\[cfg(test)]' first per file.

tasks.impl_notes: Fixed: matches.sort_by(|a,b| b.confidence.partial_cmp(...).ok_or_else(...).unwrap_or_else(...))

AddIntegrationTest: verify session CRUD operations with real SQLite database
tasks.impl_notes: Key: explicit Ok::<T, rusqlite::Error>(...) inside closures for type inference. Uses ?? at call sites for double error unwrapping.

tasks.title: Service Layer Refinement - Root Task
tasks.description: COMPLETED. All subtasks complete. Review of service layer for DI needs done. StorageService already uses DI (P1-001.8). MdnsService uses factory pattern. SapAiCoreProvider is an LLM provider. AuthManager is unused dead code. All verification gates pass.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: StorageService DI already done in P1-001.8
tasks.description: COMPLETED. StorageService was already refactored in P1-001.8. It accepts Arc<dyn SessionRepository> + Arc<dyn ProjectRepository> + StoragePool via constructor. All 16 call sites across 7 files use the new signature. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddIntegrationTest: StorageService works with injected repositories
AddUnitTest: StorageService can be instantiated with mock repositories
tasks.impl_notes: Use Arc<dyn SessionRepository> pattern for trait objects.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: MdnsService uses factory pattern - no DI needed
tasks.description: COMPLETED. MdnsService uses a factory pattern - ServiceDaemon is created internally via mdns_sd::ServiceDaemon::new(). ServerConfig is passed to start() at startup. No trait-based DI needed. This is the standard pattern for infrastructure services that manage their own resources. MdnsService is pub(crate) so it's internal to the server crate. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: MdnsService can be instantiated with mock dependencies
RunRegressionTest: MdnsService functionality unchanged
tasks.impl_notes: Factory pattern is appropriate here. Infrastructure services like mDNS daemons manage their own resources and are typically started once at application boot.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: SAP AI Core is LLM provider, not service - already uses DI
tasks.description: COMPLETED. 'SAP Service' is actually SapAiCoreProvider (crates/llm/src/sap_aicore.rs) - an LLM provider for SAP AI Core, not a service. It already uses constructor injection with ProviderConfig + SapAiCoreServiceKey. Already properly structured for DI. No architectural changes needed. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: SAP Service can be instantiated with mock dependencies
RunRegressionTest: SAP Service functionality unchanged
tasks.impl_notes: SapAiCoreProvider implements the Provider trait and uses standard constructor injection. No DI refactoring needed.

tasks.title: SKIPPED: AuthManager is unused dead code - no DI benefit
tasks.description: SKIPPED (per user decision). AuthManager (crates/auth/src/manager.rs) is defined and re-exported but NEVER used anywhere in the codebase. No call sites exist. Creating AccountRepository trait + refactoring would touch ~10 files across 5-6 crates with zero practical benefit. StorageService already uses DI (SessionRepository + ProjectRepository). AuthManager depends on concrete StorageService but AuthManager itself is dead code. User confirmed skip. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: Auth Service can be instantiated with mock dependencies
RunRegressionTest: Auth Service functionality unchanged
tasks.impl_notes: AuthManager.register/login/verify_token methods are never called. If/when AuthManager is integrated, it should accept Arc<dyn AccountRepository> for testability. For now, no action.

tasks.title: Unsafe Code Safety Documentation - Root Task
tasks.description: COMPLETED. All production unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments. tui/app.rs: removed unnecessary unsafe {} block (env::set_var is safe). server/validation.rs: added SAFETY comments to 2 raw pointer derefs in NumberValidator. plugin/lib.rs: added SAFETY comment to plugin loader call. plugin/loader.rs: already had /// # Safety on unsafe fn. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅.
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: all unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments
RunTestSuite: cargo test --all passes
tasks.impl_notes: Can run in parallel after P0. Independent of P1.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: tui/app.rs unsafe block removed (not unsafe)
tasks.description: COMPLETED. tui/app.rs had 1 production unsafe block (line 847) wrapping std::env::set_var. std::env::set_var is NOT unsafe in Rust - removed the unsafe block entirely. 2 other unsafe blocks in app.rs (lines 4671, 4684) are in #[test] module and exempt. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: all 3 unsafe blocks in app.rs have SAFETY comments
RunTestSuite: cargo test -p opencode-tui passes
tasks.impl_notes: std::env::set_var is a safe function in Rust. Wrapping it in unsafe {} was unnecessary. Removed the block entirely.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: server/validation.rs unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments
tasks.description: COMPLETED. server/validation.rs: Added SAFETY comments to 2 raw pointer dereference blocks (NumberValidator::min and NumberValidator::max). The unsafe blocks dereference *mut ValidationErrors created from &mut ValidationErrors in NumberValidator::new. SAFETY comments explain: (1) pointer validity invariant, (2) aliasing requirement (no other &mut), (3) lifetime requirement (ValidationErrors outlives validator). Also added /// # Safety doc comments on the impl block. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: all 2 unsafe blocks in validation.rs have SAFETY comments
RunTestSuite: cargo test -p opencode-server passes
tasks.impl_notes: Raw pointer deref pattern used to avoid lifetime issues in builder-style API. SAFETY comments document the invariants callers must maintain.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: plugin/loader.rs already has SAFETY docs + lib.rs call site has SAFETY comment
tasks.description: COMPLETED. plugin/loader.rs: unsafe fn load_plugin already has /// # Safety doc comment explaining ABI version requirement and Plugin trait implementation requirement. plugin/lib.rs: Added // SAFETY: comment to the unsafe {} block calling load_plugin, referencing the loader's SAFETY docs and noting that config.name was validated during discovery and library_path was verified to exist. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
test_criteria: AddRegressionTest: the 1 unsafe block in plugin/lib.rs has SAFETY comment
RunTestSuite: cargo test -p opencode-plugin passes
tasks.impl_notes: Dynamic library loading via libloading. SAFETY docs cover ABI contract and Plugin trait requirements.

tasks.title: COMPLETED: All unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments

(2) tools/registry.rs lines 1266,1321,1328,1332 in #[cfg(test)] module - exempt. (3) plugin/loader.rs: unsafe fn declarations (already have /// # Safety). (4) plugin/lib.rs:654 and server/validation.rs:237,256 - have // SAFETY: comments. All production unsafe blocks compliant. cargo build ✅ clippy ✅ test ✅
